subject: Mathematics
title: Remainders and Patterns
description: A quick, practical guide to Remainders and Patterns.

hook.text: Divide any number by 7 and the remainder is always between 0 and 6. Always.

buildup.text: These remainders form repeating cycles. Powers of 2 mod 7 go: 2, 4, 1, 2, 4, 1.

discovery.text: Finding these cycles lets you predict huge calculations without computing them.

twist.text: The last digit of any power follows a cycle too. Powers of 3 end in 3, 9, 7, 1, and repeat.

climax.text: Modular patterns simplify problems that would otherwise take millions of steps.

punchline.text: Remainders repeat, and repetition is power.

quiz.question: What pattern do remainders of powers often follow?
options: They cycle through a repeating sequence
